audience_description: HQ Subject Specialists at Eduversal HQ — gated by ch_subjects[] (math, biology, chemistry, physics, science, english, bahasa, religion). Visit partner schools across the network in their subject specialty, conduct school appraisals (school_appraisals_v2), conduct teacher appraisals in their subject across schools, lead network-wide subject communities of practice, drive Cambridge syllabus alignment, and produce HQ-level subject performance analysis. The role most exposed to network-level tacit knowledge — turnover hurts disproportionately.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikbud 6/2018
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Specialists conduct school appraisal visits as Eduversal HQ-side equivalents of supervisi role.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikdasmen 13/2025 — Pembelajaran Mendalam
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Specialists drive deep-learning approach across the subject network.

regulatory_anchors.title: PP 19/2005 jo. 13/2015 — 8 SNP
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Standar Isi, Standar Proses, Standar Penilaian — Specialist holds network-wide accountability for these in their subject.

regulatory_anchors.title: Cambridge Handbook for Centres
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Specialist anchors network-wide Cambridge subject delivery quality.

regulatory_anchors.title: Eduversal Indonesia — Academic Standards & Partner School Performance Checklist
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Network-wide weighted performance criteria — Specialist is primary auditor.

framework_anchors.cambridge_teacher_standards_2023: Specialist anchors network-wide CTS implementation in their subject (9 of 27 CTS standards by track design).
framework_anchors.appraisal_framework_v2_1: Specialist conducts cross-school teacher appraisals in their subject — primary appraiser role.
framework_anchors.school_appraisal_framework: Specialist conducts school_appraisals_v2 visits — 5 domains × 31 aspects.
framework_anchors.epdp_principal_observation: When EPDP launches, Specialist co-conducts paired Principal Leadership Observation alongside School Appraisal v2 visits.

recurring_tasks.title: Weekly review of network pacing dashboard for your subject
recurring_tasks.description: Pull the network-wide pacing dashboard for your ch_subjects[]. Identify any school more than 2 weeks behind the syllabus pacing target. Flag for outreach.
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Don't react to one school — react to patterns. If 3+ schools are slipping on the same topic, that's a network signal: examiner-report change? Resource gap? Calendar collision? Surface to your Director.

recurring_tasks.title: Weekly subject community of practice (CoP) check-in
recurring_tasks.description: Engage with the network-wide subject Subject Leader CoP (TH platform). Respond to questions on the message board, share resources, surface network-level patterns.

recurring_tasks.title: Update your IPDL with practice impact statements
recurring_tasks.description: Specialists model the IPDL discipline they expect of teachers. Update with: school visits conducted, teacher appraisals completed, resources curated, network-level patterns identified.

monthly_tasks.title: Monthly Cambridge syllabus + examiner-report review
monthly_tasks.description: Read all Cambridge updates relevant to your subject. Disseminate to Subject Leaders across the network. Update network resource library where syllabus changes affect scheme of work.

monthly_tasks.title: Monthly network-level subject analysis to your Director
monthly_tasks.description: First Monday: brief your Director (Primary or Secondary, matching your subject's stage range) on subject performance across the network. Top 3 strengths, top 3 risks, recommended HQ actions.

termly_tasks.title: Lead termly Subject Leader Network Workshop
termly_tasks.description: Convene all Subject Leaders across the network for a half-day workshop. Theme: data-driven Problem of Practice. Outcome: shared resources + cross-school collaborative action.

tasks.title: Co-deliver annual Eduversal School Leadership Workshop
tasks.description: Specialist contributes the subject-specific track at the network workshop. Update partner schools on syllabus changes, pacing targets, resource updates, network priorities.

tasks.title: Publish annual subject network priorities for the year
tasks.description: Document for all Subject Leaders: network priorities in your subject for the year, expected pacing, resource library updates, key Cambridge changes.

4-13.phase: September-November — Sem-1 school appraisal visit window

tasks.title: Conduct Sem-1 school appraisal visits in your subject
tasks.description: Visit each partner school offering your subject for the school_appraisals_v2 cycle. 5 domains × 31 aspects rubric. Co-conduct teacher appraisals during the same visit. Under EPDP, also conduct paired Principal Leadership Observation. Single trip, multiple deliverables.

tasks.title: Maintain visit-trip log + expense reports
tasks.description: Visit logistics, travel, expenses logged within 5 working days of return. Visit findings shared with Director within 7 days.

tasks.title: Cambridge Oct/Nov entry signoff for your subject network-wide
tasks.description: Cross-check Cambridge entries across all partner schools offering your subject. Flag anomalies to Cambridge Coordinators. Aggregate any candidacy concerns to Director.

tasks.title: Lead EASE I cycle for your subject
tasks.description: Subject-side ownership: paper finalisation, marking scheme distribution, post-cycle network analysis. Cross-school comparative report to Directors.

tasks.title: Network-wide Cambridge Oct/Nov results analysis for your subject
tasks.description: Aggregate results across all partner schools. Identify network-level strengths and concerns. Brief Director. Update scheme-of-work guidance where examiner reports indicate.

27-32.phase: February-April — Sem-2 school appraisal visit window

tasks.title: Conduct Sem-2 school appraisal visits in your subject
tasks.description: Same protocol as Sem-1. Compare against Sem-1 visit findings — what's improved, what's stuck.

tasks.title: Lead EASE II cycle for your subject
tasks.description: Same protocol as EASE I. Sem-2 cycle 2 paper. Year-on-year network comparison.

tasks.title: Annual subject network performance report
tasks.description: Comprehensive year-end report: subject-network results trend, school-by-school comparative, top performing practices for next-year propagation, recommendations to Directors.

tasks.title: Year-end resource library refresh for your subject
tasks.description: Refresh the network resource library (pacing docs, exemplar schemes of work, exemplar lesson plans, marking scheme deep-dives, examiner report syntheses). Ready for next year's intake.

induction_overlay.applies_when: users.induction_status in {pre_arrival, network_sense_making, coaching_apprenticeship, solo_practice, strategic_contribution}

extra_recurring_tasks_first_year.title: Weekly mentor-Specialist meeting (first 18 weeks)
extra_recurring_tasks_first_year.description: Paired with a Level 4 (Lead) Specialist mentor in adjacent subject. Weekly 30-minute meeting through Stages 2-3. Bring observations, dilemmas, questions on visiting protocol.

extra_recurring_tasks_first_year.title: Specialist 10-walkthrough cycle with mentor
extra_recurring_tasks_first_year.description: Stages 2-3 of induction: 10 walkthroughs across partner schools jointly with mentor. Build the school-visit muscle. Calibrate observation against mentor's reads.

extra_recurring_tasks_first_year.title: Weekly induction journal entry
extra_recurring_tasks_first_year.description: Private to mentor only (NN2). What surprised me about the network this week? What pattern is emerging?

extra_recurring_tasks_first_year.title: Q4 windowed evaluation (Stage 5)
extra_recurring_tasks_first_year.description: End of induction year: formal evaluation by HQ Director using Specialist competency framework. Formative-only — feeds coaching, not appraisal (NN1).

reads_from: All Cambridge bulletins relevant to ch_subjects[]

provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge Teacher Standards 2023 (9/27), Cambridge School Leader Standards 2023 (anchored), Eduversal Specialist Competency Framework, school-appraisal-framework.json, appraisal-framework-v2.json
provenance.operational_pattern_source: Eduversal-network role definition. Specialist visits OWN the school appraisal cycle — Semesta's HoD/Coord patterns are the school-side mirror, this is the HQ-side mirror.
